markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Feed code navigation and diagnostics into embedded Monaco without requiring external editor services. This MT belongs to CodeIndexingAndNavigation. Index code structure and expose precise backend navigation for agents.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Code navigation must use Handshake-managed parsers/adapters and store spans/citations in PostgreSQL.
Tree-sitter is the first parser family; LSP/SCIP-style precision is a Handshake-owned import/projection path, not an outside service requirement.
scope.expected_behavior: MonacoCodeLensBridge leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: MonacoCodeLensBridge is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 working tree: code-index implementation hardened after EventLedger JSONB idempotency false-conflict; real PostgreSQL tests green (knowledge_code_index_tests 22, knowledge_code_index:: lib 82, knowledge_code_nav_api_tests 2) plus MT-109 exact stress 25/25; adversarial re-review PASS 2026-06-14.

concerns: Scalability/N+1: build_monaco_payload loads ALL workspace symbol entities (monaco_bridge.rs:128 list_knowledge_entities_by_kind) and filters by the four `{lang}:{path}#` prefixes in Rust; then for EACH matching symbol it runs list_knowledge_entity_span_ids + list_knowledge_edges_for_entity + (per documents edge) get_knowledge_entity. For one file this is bounded by file symbol count, but the initial load is O(workspace_symbols), so rendering a lens in a 100k-symbol workspace transfers the whole symbol table per request. No SQL pushdown on the file prefix.

Prefix filter relies on entity_key shape: matches `rust:{path}#` / `typescript:{path}#` / `tsx:{path}#` / `javascript:{path}#`. The trailing '#' prevents a sibling-path false match (rust:src/foo# vs rust:src/foobar#), verified - so this is safe, not a bug, but it is a hard-coded language list: a 5th language added later silently drops from the lens until this list is updated.

caller_count counts incoming references edges across the WHOLE workspace (monaco_bridge.rs:163) but the `references` ranges shown are only those whose evidence spans live in this file (filtered via span_by_id, monaco_bridge.rs:167); so caller_count can exceed the number of reference ranges displayed - a consumer reconciling the two will see a mismatch (count > shown). Cosmetic but unexplained in the payload.
evasion_probes: VERIFIED staleness IS attached here (MonacoCodeLensPayload.staleness, monaco_bridge.rs:91-105) via evaluate_staleness against the caller's content_hash/parser_version; no-code_file-row -> MarkedStale. This route is the ONE place the 'never serve stale silently' guarantee is honored (cf. MT-106 where the symbol routes omit it). Integration test asserts staleness.state == 'fresh' on a freshly-indexed file.

Confirmed this is BACKEND SHAPE only (typed payload + query); no React/Monaco runtime wiring, matching the contract's explicit 'not the React wiring (that lands in a later GUI group)'. No GUI surface to visually debug for this MT - correctly scoped.
Confirmed deterministic ordering (entries sorted by definition.start_line then symbol_key, references sorted+dedup) - stable payload across runs.
No panic: all span/edge lookups Option/Result-guarded; missing definition span -> LineRange{0,0} default.

hardening_closed.note: Closed for READY_FOR_VALIDATION. Prior HARDEN_RECOMMENDED concerns remain recorded in adversarial_code_review and are non-blocking follow-up.

evidence: EventLedger idempotency false-conflict root cause confirmed as PostgreSQL JSONB payload numeric normalization while canonical payload_hash matched.
append_kernel_event idempotency now compares canonical metadata plus payload_hash instead of raw JSONB Value equality; detectors accept enriched conflict diagnostics.
Adversarial re-review PASS: no blocking findings for MT-101/102/107/109/110/111/112 readiness.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-109 requires a backend MonacoCodeLensBridge payload that feeds code navigation/diagnostics into embedded Monaco from PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority (no external editor service), with staleness flagging and validator-readable real-PG proof. The code delivers a complete, real implementation: a typed payload built from the Postgres knowledge graph with attached staleness, a receipted nav API route with path-traversal guard, DB-side path pushdown after hardening, and runtime tests plus a frontend Playwright spec that exercise it against real Handshake-managed PostgreSQL.
findings: monaco_bridge.rs:76-245 build_monaco_payload reads entities/spans/edges from PostgreSQL via KnowledgeStore and never re-parses; emits typed MonacoCodeLensPayload {workspace_id, relative_path, staleness, truncated, entries} - a derived projection over Postgres authority, matching Master Spec v02.194 2.3.13.11 (EditorCodeNode/Monaco-backed; projections-only; backend nav APIs).

monaco_bridge.rs:96-110 attaches StalenessVerdict via evaluate_staleness against the caller content_hash/parser_version; a missing knowledge_code_files row defaults to MarkedStale - the 'never serve a stale lens as authoritative' guarantee is honored here.

api/knowledge_code_nav.rs:699-751 file_lens exposes GET /knowledge/code/files/:path/lens with is_safe_relative_path traversal rejection (line 712/829) and typed nav + quiet receipts (record_nav_receipt/record_quiet_nav_receipt).

MT-109 hardening commit b63c1e84 replaces the load-all-workspace-symbols DoS with DB-side path pushdown: lookup_code_symbols (storage/knowledge.rs:6837-6890) uses a parameterized, LIKE-escaped %:{path}#% segment match (escape_like, line 6896); monaco_bridge.rs:134-157 re-checks the exact {lang}:{path}# prefix in Rust to keep the language boundary tight.

Real-PG proof: tests/knowledge_pg_support.rs header + resolver mandate REAL PostgreSQL only (POSTGRES_TEST_URL/DATABASE_URL/managed ManagedPostgres::ensure_running; explicit 'NO SQLite, in-memory, or mock fallback', SKIP loudly when PG binaries absent). knowledge_code_index_tests.rs:1277-1503 (mt109_monaco_payload_has_entries_with_ranges, mt107 staleness fresh/SourceChanged, mt107 reindex) drive build_monaco_payload end-to-end; knowledge_code_nav_api_tests.rs:397-422 drives the /lens API and asserts staleness 'fresh' + entries + receipt; tests/dependency_policy/mt249_code_intelligence.spec.ts:219 confirms the /lens route is consumed by real embedded Monaco offline.

No placeholder/deferred-live escape: grep for todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/Unavailable/'deferred to follow-on'/'pending MT-'/stub across monaco_bridge.rs and knowledge_code_nav.rs returned nothing on the required path. All span/edge lookups are Option/Result-guarded (no panic).

Non-blocking quality follow-ups (recorded under hardening_pending, do NOT affect verdict): residual per-symbol N+1 span/edge fetch inside the loop; whole-workspace caller_count vs file-filtered reference-range cosmetic mismatch; hard-coded 4-language prefix list (rust/typescript/tsx/javascript) silently drops a future 5th language until updated.
Aside (paperwork, non-blocking): packet spec_anchors cite v02.192; current bundle is v02.194 - same section numbers resolve and align.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
